title: leontodon hispidus subsp. opimus
intro: Leontodon hispidus subsp. opimus, also known as Rough Hawk's Beard, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a digestive aid and to treat various gastrointestinal issues, including constipation and indigestion. The plant's roots and leaves are rich in antioxidants and have been found to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome and other health conditions. In herbal preparations, the roots and leaves of Leontodon hispidus subsp. opimus are often dried and used as a tea or tincture, or added to salves and ointments for topical application.
